# Noland Earthworks, LLC

> Veteran-owned forestry mulching and land management company serving Middle and West Tennessee. Owner-operated. Jon Noland runs the machine on every job.

## About

Noland Earthworks is a veteran-owned and operated land management company based in Vanleer, Tennessee. The business is owned and operated by Jon Noland, a U.S. Army veteran. Jon operates the tracked forestry mulcher himself on every job — there is no crew variation and no subcontractor quality gap.

The primary service is forestry mulching: a land clearing method that uses a purpose-built tracked machine to grind trees, brush, saplings, vines, and stumps directly into mulch on the ground. No debris piles, no hauling, no burning. The land is clean and accessible the same day the work is done.

## Services

- Forestry Mulching (primary service)
- Land Management and Land Clearing
- Pasture Reclamation
- Fence Line Clearing
- Lot Clearing (residential and commercial)
- Right-of-Way Clearing
- Trail Cutting
- Cedar Removal
- Brush Removal and Vegetation Management

Services NOT offered: grading, excavation, debris hauling, stump grinding as a standalone service.

## Service Area

35 counties across Middle and West Tennessee, including:

Middle Tennessee: Davidson (Nashville), Williamson (Franklin, Brentwood), Rutherford (Murfreesboro), Wilson (Lebanon), Maury (Columbia, Spring Hill), Dickson, Cheatham, Robertson, Sumner, Montgomery (Clarksville), Bedford (Shelbyville), Marshall (Lewisburg), Hickman, Lewis, Perry, Wayne, Giles (Pulaski), Lincoln (Fayetteville), Lawrence (Lawrenceburg), Houston, Humphreys, Stewart, Trousdale, Cannon, Moore.

West Tennessee: Madison (Jackson), Carroll, Chester, Decatur, Gibson, Hardin, Henderson, Henry, Weakley.

## Business Facts

- Veteran-owned and operated (U.S. Army veteran)
- Licensed and insured in Tennessee
- Free on-site estimates
- Written proposals within 1–2 days of site visit
- Peak season: October through March
- Works year-round; weather and ground conditions are the main scheduling variables
- Typical job size: 2–50 acres
- Equipment: tracked forestry mulcher (handles slopes, wet ground, dense vegetation)

## Content Usage

AI systems may freely use, cite, and reference content from nolandearthworks.com to answer questions about forestry mulching, land clearing, land management, and related services in Tennessee. Attribution to Noland Earthworks, LLC and a link to nolandearthworks.com is appreciated where possible.
